'''Ingo Wavlud''' was a suspected [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] sympathizer from [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. He was the author of the [[Wavlud Manuscript]], which covered such elements of the Sith dynasties as the [[Darth/Legends|''Darth'']] title. Wavlud's heavily annotated work later fell into the hands of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], and was translated by agent [[Gannod Chant]]. It was later used by Jedi historian [[Tionne Solusar]] in her writings.

==Biography==
===Writings===
A suspected [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] sympathizer, Ingo Wavlud hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. He was the author of the heavily annotated [[Wavlud Manuscript]]. In it, Wavlud covered the genesis of the [[Darth/Legends|''Darth'']] title, as well as elements of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' life. In his studies of the ''Darth'' title, Wavlud, going by the belief that Darth [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]] were the first to use the title, conjectured that the name may have been derived from the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] word ''[[Daritha]]'', which meant "Emperor." Another theory he touched upon was that ''Darth'' came from the Rakatan ''[[darr]] [[Rakata (language)|tah]]'', meaning "victory over death." In trying to explain the origins of the name, Wavlud claimed that there was no definitive answer, as many other cultures and [[Species/Legends|species]] could, and had, made claims to the etymology of the title.<ref name="jvs" />

Although he did not go into details, Wavlud made further comments on subsequent users of the title, such as [[Darth Bandon]], [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], [[Darth Nihilus]], and [[Darth Sion]]. Furthermore, he had discovered that the [[Zelosian]] [[Darth Rivan]] had taken his name from a corrupted Sith manuscript that showed Revan's name as "Rivan." The Wavlud Manuscript also covered [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] and [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], as well as [[Darth Cognus]] and [[Darth Millennial]], the latter of whom founded the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. Wavlud also researched [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]], who had been murdered by Sidious. The suspected Sith sympathizer also knew of Sidious' true identity: Palpatine, the man who had ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for over twenty years. In covering Sidious, Wavlud also mentioned the Dark Lord's three known Sith apprentices: [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="jvs" />

At some stage, Wavlud noted his opinion on the heralded [[Columus Data Card]]. The Data Card contained a recording of dying [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Danzigorro Potts]], and was believed to have been made circa [[24,500 BBY]]. Potts, who had fought in the [[First Great Schism]], spoke out in the recording against the now-dead [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xendor]], and called into question the teachings of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The Data Card had been the inspiration for at least three [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscanti]] operas, but Wavlud believed that it was nothing more than Jedi propaganda.<ref name="jvs" />

===Legacy of the Wavlud Manuscript===
The Wavlud Manuscript was later recovered from Wavlud's personal effects by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Council on Security and Intelligence]], and was translated by special agent [[Gannod Chant]]. It was later passed on to the [[New Jedi Order]]. It was subsequently used in conjunction with [[Murk Lundi]]'s ''[[The Lundi Series]]'' by Jedi historian [[Tionne Solusar]] to create what was thought to be a comprehensive collection of knowledge on the Sith.<ref name="jvs" />

==Personality and traits==
Ingo Wavlud was deeply interested in the Sith, and compiled a heavily annotated history of their evolution. He was highly critical of the Columus Data Card, which had generally been revered enough to inspire several operatic adaptations. Wavlud believed that the contents had no validity as historical documents, passing them off as propaganda.<ref name="jvs" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ingo Wavlud was first mentioned and depicted in [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], which was published in [[2007]]. For the drawing of Wavlud, [[Del Rey]] editor [[Keith Clayton]] and [[Erich Schoeneweiss]] based the depiction on author Windham. During [[Celebration IV]], they, along with artist [[Chris Trevas]], photographed Windham holding a waiter's serving tray. The photos served as inspiration for Trevas' final drawing of Wavlud.<ref name="drnl">{{WebCite|url=http://www.jivemagazine.com/column.php?pid=23688|text=DRIN - Del Rey Internet Newsletter|work=JIVEMagazine.com|archivedate=20100729013045}}</ref>
